and the relief sought is limited to a type to which [ F38 that Convention ] applies, but the court may at any time direct that the financial statement of either party shall be verified by a statement of truth. ]


(3) Where a party was unavoidably prevented from sending any document required by the financial statement, that party must at the earliest opportunity—


(4) No disclosure or inspection of documents may be requested or given between the filing of the application for a financial remedy and the first appointment, except—


(Rule 21,1 explains what is meant by disclosure and inspection.)


(5) Not less than 14 days before the hearing of the first appointment, each party must file with the court and serve on the other party—


(6) Not less than 14 days before the hearing of the first appointment, the applicant must file with the court and serve on the respondent confirmation—


Textual Amendments


F33 Words in rule 9.14(2)(a) substituted (6.4.2012) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2012 (S.I. 2012/679) , rules 1 , 16(a) (with rule 30 )


F34 Rule 9.14(2ZA) inserted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/667) , rules 1 , 8 (with rule 45 )


F35 Words in rule 9.14(2ZA) omitted (31.12.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure Rules 2010 and Court of Protection Rules 2017 (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/517) , regs. 1(1) , 8(5)(a) (with reg. 23 ) (as amended by S.I. 2020/1493 , regs. 1(1) , 8(2)(b) ); 2020 c. 1 , Sch. 5 para. 1(1)


F36 Rule 9.14(2A) substituted (20.12.2012) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 4) Rules 2012 (S.I. 2012/2806) , rules 1 , 9(a)

metadata.title: Part 9 — Applications for a Financial Remedy
